walkthrough.title: Place the Map
walkthrough.text: Cthulhu Wars uses a two-piece map representing the Eastern and Western Hemispheres. Each piece is double-sided: one side for 3-player games and one for 5-player games. For a 3-player game, place both hemispheres with the 3-player side face up, giving you 13 total Areas. For a 4-player game, use one 3-player hemisphere and one 5-player hemisphere for 17 total Areas. We recommend the Eastern Hemisphere on the 5-player side and the Western on the 3-player side for your first 4-player game. Remember: the far right of the map connects to the far left because the Earth is round.

walkthrough.title: Place the Ritual of Annihilation Track
walkthrough.text: Find the Ritual of Annihilation Track that matches your player count and place it at one end of the table. Put the remaining tracks back in the box. Place the Ritual of Annihilation Marker pointed at the lowest number, which is 5. This track determines the cost of performing Rituals during the Doom Phase and will escalate as players perform Rituals throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Place the Doom Track and Dice
walkthrough.text: Place the Doom Track next to the Ritual of Annihilation Track. This is where you will track each faction's progress toward victory. Place the Gates and all dice within easy reach of all players. The dice are six-sided and used for combat resolution throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Elder Sign Tokens
walkthrough.text: Place all 36 Elder Sign Trophy Tokens into the cloth bag and shake them up. Alternatively, you can turn all tokens face down on the table with the Elder Sign symbol on top and mix them up. These tokens are worth 1, 2, or 3 Doom points each. There are 18 worth 1 point, 12 worth 2 points, and 6 worth 3 points. Players will earn these throughout the game and can reveal them at any time to gain Doom.

walkthrough.title: Hand Out Hint Cards
walkthrough.text: Give each player a Hint Card. These cards provide a quick reference for game mechanics and can be especially helpful for newer players learning the flow of the game.

walkthrough.title: Choose Factions
walkthrough.text: Each player selects a Faction to play. The four core factions are Great Cthulhu, Black Goat, Crawling Chaos, and Yellow Sign. You can choose freely or draw Faction Glyph tokens randomly from a cup. If there is a dispute, let the younger players choose first. Each Faction plays very differently, so take a moment to look over the Faction Cards before deciding.

walkthrough.title: Distribute Faction Components
walkthrough.text: Each player takes their Faction Card, their six Spellbooks, and all plastic figures of their faction's color. If Yellow Sign is in play, that player also takes the 12 Desecration Markers. All other Desecration Markers go back in the box. Place your Spellbooks and unused figures near your Faction Card as your Pool. Feel free to read your Spellbooks now to start planning your strategy.

walkthrough.title: Set Doom and Power Markers
walkthrough.text: Place each player's Doom Marker on the zero space of the Doom Track. Then each player places their Power Marker at the 8 space on the Power Track printed on their Faction Card. Every player starts with 8 Power for the first Action Phase.

walkthrough.title: Place Starting Units on the Map
walkthrough.text: Check your Faction Card for your Start Area, which is marked with your Faction's Glyph on the map. Place a Gate in that Area. Place one of your 6 Acolyte Cultists on top of the Gate to Control it. Place your remaining 5 Acolyte Cultists in the same Area. All players start with a single Controlled Gate and 6 Acolytes in their home territory.

walkthrough.title: Determine the First Player
walkthrough.text: When Great Cthulhu is in the game, that player is always the First Player at the start. Give them the First Player Token. If Great Cthulhu is not in the game, choose the First Player by any method you wish. The rulebook recommends going alphabetically by Faction name. The First Player sets the token with either the clockwise or counterclockwise side face up, which determines the direction of play for all phases until the next Determine First Player Phase. You are now ready to begin the Action Phase.

summary.title: Place the Map
bullets: Two-piece map (Eastern and Western Hemisphere), printed on both sides
3-player: both 3-player sides face up (13 Areas)
4-player: one 3-player side + one 5-player side (17 Areas)
Far right connects to far left (Earth is round)

summary.title: Place the Ritual of Annihilation Track
bullets: Select the track matching your player count
Place Ritual Marker at the lowest number (5)
Return unused tracks to the box

summary.title: Place the Doom Track and Dice
bullets: Place Doom Track next to Ritual of Annihilation Track
Place Gates and dice within reach of all players

summary.title: Prepare the Elder Sign Tokens
bullets: Place all 36 Elder Sign Trophy Tokens in the cloth bag
Tokens are worth 1 (x18), 2 (x12), or 3 (x6) Doom points each
Players earn and reveal these throughout the game

summary.title: Hand Out Hint Cards
bullets: Give each player 1 Hint Card for quick reference

summary.title: Choose Factions
bullets: 4 core factions: Great Cthulhu, Black Goat, Crawling Chaos, Yellow Sign
Choose freely or draw Faction Glyph tokens randomly
Each Faction has unique units, abilities, and Spellbooks

summary.title: Distribute Faction Components
bullets: Each player takes: Faction Card, 6 Spellbooks, all plastic figures of their color
Yellow Sign also takes 12 Desecration Markers
Unused figures and Spellbooks form your Pool

summary.title: Set Doom and Power Markers
bullets: Place Doom Marker at 0 on the Doom Track
Place Power Marker at 8 on Faction Card's Power Track

summary.title: Place Starting Units on the Map
bullets: Find your Start Area (marked with your Faction's Glyph)
Place 1 Gate in your Start Area
Place 1 Acolyte Cultist on top of the Gate (Controlling it)
Place remaining 5 Acolyte Cultists in the same Area

summary.title: Determine the First Player
bullets: Great Cthulhu's player is always First Player if in the game
Otherwise, choose by any method (alphabetical by Faction recommended)
First Player places token clockwise or counterclockwise to set play direction

walkthrough.title: Game Overview and Phases
walkthrough.text: Cthulhu Wars is played in a repeating cycle of four phases: 1. Action Phase, 2. Gather Power Phase, 3. Determine First Player Phase, and 4. Doom Phase. After setup, play begins with the Action Phase and proceeds through each phase, looping back to Phase 1 after Phase 4. A typical game lasts four to six cycles. The goal is to have the highest Doom on the Doom Track while having placed all 6 Spellbooks on your Faction Card when the game ends.

walkthrough.title: The Action Phase: Overview
walkthrough.text: The Action Phase is where most of the game happens. Starting with the First Player, each player takes a single Action on their turn, then the next player goes. This continues around the table until all players have acted, then it loops again. Each Action costs Power, which you spend by lowering your Power Marker on your Faction Card. If you cannot afford an Action, you cannot take it. If you have Power but do not want to act, you may voluntarily drop to 0 Power. Once you hit 0, you are skipped until the Gather Power Phase. The Action Phase ends when all players are at 0 Power.

walkthrough.title: Four Types of Actions
walkthrough.text: There are four categories of Actions. Common Actions are available to all factions and there are seven of them. Unique Actions come from your Faction's unique ability, your Spellbooks, or your Great Old One's special ability. Spellbook Requirement Actions are printed on your Faction Card and can only be done once each per game. Unlimited Actions are special because you can perform them before or after your single Action each turn. On your turn, you take any number of Unlimited Actions, then exactly one Common, Unique, or Spellbook Requirement Action, then any number of Unlimited Actions again.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Recruit Cultist
walkthrough.text: Cost: 1 Power. Place one Acolyte Cultist from your Pool onto the map in any Area where you already have a Unit. If you have no Units anywhere on the map, you can place the Cultist in any Area. You may only recruit one Cultist per Recruit action. Cultists are critical because they are your primary source of Power during the Gather Power Phase and the only units that can Control Gates.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Summon Monster
walkthrough.text: Cost: varies by Monster type (listed on your Faction Card). Place one Monster from your Pool onto the map in an Area where you Control a Gate. You may only summon one Monster per action. Each Faction has unique Monster types with different costs, Combat ratings, and special abilities. You are limited by the number of figures in your Pool.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Awaken Great Old One
walkthrough.text: Cost: varies (specific steps on your Faction Card). Your Faction Card lists a multi-step process to bring your Great Old One into play. Follow all steps as a single Action. Once Awakened, your Great Old One's Special Ability becomes available. If your Great Old One is Killed in battle, it returns to your Pool and you must Awaken it again, paying the full cost. Most factions have one Great Old One, and each is uniquely powerful.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Create Gate
walkthrough.text: Cost: 3 Power. You must have a Cultist in the Area, and there cannot already be a Gate there. Place a Gate in the Area and immediately place your Cultist on top to Control it. Only one Gate can ever exist in any Area. Gates are extremely valuable because they provide both Power during the Gather Power Phase and Doom during the Doom Phase.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Move
walkthrough.text: Cost: 1 Power per Unit moved. Spend as much Power as you wish. For each Power spent, move one Unit from its current Area to an adjacent Area. You can move Units from different Areas to different destinations in any combination. Each Unit may only move once per Move Action, but you can move as many Units as you can afford. This is the only Common Action that lets you act on multiple Units at once.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Battle
walkthrough.text: Cost: 1 Power. Choose an Area where you and an enemy Faction both have Units. If multiple enemies are present, you choose which one to fight. You must have at least 1 Combat among your Units to declare battle. You may only battle once per Battle Action, in one Area. Battle has its own detailed resolution sequence covered in a later step.

walkthrough.title: Common Action: Capture Cultist
walkthrough.text: Cost: 1 Power. If you have a Monster or Great Old One in the same Area as an enemy Cultist, you can Capture that Cultist, but only if the target is unprotected. The pecking order is: Great Old Ones protect against enemy Monsters, and Monsters protect against enemy Monsters. Only a Great Old One can Capture past an enemy Monster, and even then, the target's own Great Old One blocks it. The Captured Cultist goes on your Faction Card. During the Gather Power Phase, you return it and gain 1 Power. This denies your opponent both Power and Gate control.

walkthrough.title: Unlimited Actions
walkthrough.text: There are two Unlimited Actions. Control or Abandon Gate costs 0 Power and lets you place a Cultist onto an Abandoned Gate to Control it, or remove a Cultist from a Gate to Abandon it. Unlimited Battle costs 1 Power and requires all 6 Spellbooks on your Faction Card. It lets you initiate additional battles before or after your normal Action, but you can still only battle once per Area per turn. Unlimited Actions can be performed any number of times on your turn, before or after your single main Action.

walkthrough.title: Battle Resolution: Pre-Battle
walkthrough.text: Battle proceeds through four steps. First is Pre-Battle Abilities. The attacker uses all their Pre-Battle abilities first, in any order. Then the defender uses theirs. Some abilities Eliminate or remove Units before dice are even rolled. If all Units on one side are removed during Pre-Battle, the battle ends immediately. A key example is Great Cthulhu's Devour, which Eliminates an enemy Monster or Cultist before combat dice are rolled.

walkthrough.title: Battle Resolution: Rolling Dice
walkthrough.text: Each side totals the Combat rating of all their Units in the battle. Roll that many dice. Count sixes as Kill results. Count fours and fives as Pain results. Ones, twos, and threes are misses. Both sides roll simultaneously. Combat ratings are found on your Faction Card. For example, Crawling Chaos Acolytes have 0 Combat, and Hunting Horrors have 2 Combat each.

walkthrough.title: Battle Resolution: Assigning Results
walkthrough.text: After rolling, each player assigns the enemy's results to their own Units. First assign all Kill results. Each Kill assigned to a Unit will remove it from the map. Then use any Post-Battle abilities, which can modify results. After all Kills and any Eliminations are assigned, assign Pain results. Pained Units will be forced to retreat. Excess Kills or Pains beyond your remaining Units are ignored. You choose which of your own Units take each result.

walkthrough.title: Battle Resolution: Applying Results
walkthrough.text: Eliminations and Kills remove Units from the map back to their Pool. Apply these before Pains. The attacker applies their Pains first, then the defender. Pained Units retreat to an adjacent Area that does not contain any Units belonging to the enemy you just battled. If no valid retreat Area exists, you must Eliminate one of your Pained Units instead, and the rest stay put. Clever attackers can surround an Area before battling to deny retreat paths and force extra Eliminations.

walkthrough.title: Spellbooks and How to Earn Them
walkthrough.text: Each Faction Card has six Spellbook Requirement slots. When you fulfill a requirement, you must place any Spellbook from your Pool onto that slot. You choose which Spellbook goes on which slot. Once earned, a Spellbook stays on your card permanently, even if the requirement is no longer met. You can earn multiple Spellbooks simultaneously if you meet multiple requirements at once. Earning all 6 Spellbooks is essential because you cannot win without them, and they grant you powerful abilities including Unlimited Battle.

walkthrough.title: Gather Power Phase
walkthrough.text: After the Action Phase, all players simultaneously earn new Power. You gain 1 Power per Cultist in play, 2 Power per Gate you Control, 1 Power per Abandoned Gate on the map (everyone gets this), and 1 Power per Captured Cultist you return to its owner. Then check for any special abilities that grant Power. After tallying, apply the Minimum Power Rule: if your Power is less than half the highest player's Power, increase it to half that amount, rounded up. This prevents anyone from being completely crippled.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player Phase
walkthrough.text: The player with the most Power becomes the new First Player and takes the First Player Token. If tied, the previous First Player decides who among the tied players gets it. The new First Player chooses whether to set the token clockwise or counterclockwise, determining the direction of play for all phases until the next time this phase occurs.

walkthrough.title: Doom Phase: Doom Track Advancement
walkthrough.text: The Doom Phase has two steps. First, all players simultaneously advance their Doom Marker on the Doom Track by the number of Gates they Control. If you Control three Gates, you advance three spaces. Abandoned Gates give no Doom. This is your baseline Doom income each cycle and is why controlling Gates is so important.

walkthrough.title: Doom Phase: Ritual of Annihilation
walkthrough.text: Starting with the First Player and proceeding in turn order, each player chooses whether to perform a Ritual of Annihilation. To perform one: spend Power equal to the current Ritual Marker position (starts at 5, can go up to 10), advance the Ritual Marker one step (increasing cost for later players), gain Doom equal to your Controlled Gates again (doubling your Doom this phase), and earn one Elder Sign per Faction Great Old One you have in play. Performing Rituals is essential for winning, but the Power cost leaves you vulnerable in the next Action Phase.

walkthrough.title: Instant Death and Elder Signs
walkthrough.text: If the Ritual Marker reaches Instant Death on the track, the game ends at the end of that Doom Phase. All remaining players may still perform their Ritual if they have not yet had the chance. Elder Signs are hidden tokens worth 1, 2, or 3 Doom. You can reveal them at any time to gain Doom, but most players save them until the end to conceal their true score. When the game ends, all players reveal all their Elder Signs before determining the winner.

walkthrough.title: Victory Conditions
walkthrough.text: The game ends when any player reaches 30 or more Doom, or when the Ritual Marker hits Instant Death. After everyone reveals their Elder Signs and adds that Doom, the player with the highest Doom who has all 6 Spellbooks wins. If the highest-Doom player does not have 6 Spellbooks, the win goes to whoever has the most Doom among players who do have all 6. If tied, it is a shared victory. If nobody has all 6 Spellbooks, humanity wins and all players lose together.

walkthrough.title: Great Cthulhu Faction Overview
walkthrough.text: Great Cthulhu's faction is focused on destruction. Units include 6 Acolytes, 4 Deep Ones (cost 1, Combat 1), 2 Shoggoths (cost 2, Combat 2), 2 Starspawn (cost 3, Combat 3-6 depending on Spellbooks), and Cthulhu himself (cost 10, Combat 6). Cthulhu's unique faction ability is Immortal: when Cthulhu is Killed, you immediately gain an Elder Sign and can re-Awaken him for only 4 Power. Key Spellbooks include Absorb (Shoggoths absorb allied Units for extra dice), Devolve (replace a Cultist with a Deep One reactively), Dreams (steal an enemy Cultist to your Pool), Regenerate (Starspawn reduce Kills to Pains), Submerge (move your army to any ocean), and Y'ha Nthlei (build Gates in ocean Areas). Cthulhu starts in the South Pacific.

walkthrough.title: Crawling Chaos Faction Overview
walkthrough.text: Crawling Chaos excels at harassment and opportunism. Units include 6 Acolytes, 3 Nightgaunts (cost 1, Combat 0), 2 Flying Polyps (cost 2, Combat 1), 2 Hunting Horrors (cost 3, Combat 2), and Nyarlathotep (cost 10, Combat equal to number of enemy factions with units in his Area times the number of your Spellbooks). The unique ability is Flight, letting your Nightgaunts and Hunting Horrors move to any Area for 1 Power instead of only adjacent ones. Key Spellbooks include Abduct (Nightgaunts Eliminate enemy Cultists in Pre-Battle), Emissary of the Outer Gods (redirect a Kill from Nyarlathotep to a friendly Cultist), Invisibility (exempt a Polyp from battle results), Madness (you choose where all Pained Units retreat), Seek and Destroy (send a Hunting Horror into any battle for free), and Thousand Forms (steal Power from others). Crawling Chaos starts in the North Atlantic.

walkthrough.title: Black Goat Faction Overview
walkthrough.text: Black Goat emphasizes occupation, expansion, and cheap units. Units include 6 Acolytes, 3 Ghouls (cost 1, Combat 0), 2 Fungi from Yuggoth (cost 2, Combat 1), 4 Dark Young (cost 3, Combat 2), and Shub-Niggurath (cost 8, Combat varies). The unique ability is The Thousand Young, which reduces the cost of all your Monsters by 1. Key Spellbooks include Avatar (move Shub-Niggurath to any Area and swap an enemy Cultist to her old Area), Blood Sacrifice (Eliminate your own Cultist during the Doom Phase to earn an Elder Sign), Frenzy (all your Cultists gain Combat 1), Ghroth (roll dice to Eliminate enemy Cultists across the map), Necrophagy (after any battle, send Ghouls into the Area to add Pain results), and Red Sign (Dark Young can Control Gates instead of Cultists). Black Goat starts in Europe.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Sign Faction Overview
walkthrough.text: Yellow Sign plays a nomadic, disruptive game. Units include 6 Acolytes, 6 Undead (cost 1, Combat 0), 2 Byakhee (cost 1, Combat special), and the King in Yellow (cost 4, Combat 0) plus Hastur (cost 10, Combat varies). The unique ability is Desecrate, which lets you place a Desecration Marker and summon an Undead when you Desecrate an Area. Key Spellbooks include He Who is Not to be Named (Hastur teleports to any Area and you take a second Action), Passion (gain 1 Power when an enemy enters an Area with your Units), Screaming Dead (take a second different Action after the King in Yellow acts), Shriek of the Byakhee (add Byakhee from Pool to an Area with your Great Old One), Third Eye (earn an Elder Sign when Desecrating with both Great Old Ones in play), Vengeance (Hastur assigns enemy Kills rather than the enemy choosing), and Zingaya (Eliminate an enemy Cultist and replace with an Undead). Yellow Sign starts in Asia.

summary.title: Game Overview and Phases
bullets: 4 phases repeating: Action, Gather Power, Determine First Player, Doom
Typical game: 4-6 full cycles through all phases
Win: highest Doom + all 6 Spellbooks when game ends

summary.title: The Action Phase: Overview
bullets: Players take turns clockwise/counterclockwise from First Player
Each turn: spend Power to perform 1 Action
Phase ends when all players are at 0 Power
Can voluntarily drop to 0 to pass; once at 0, you are skipped

summary.title: Four Types of Actions
bullets: Common Actions: 7 options available to all factions
Unique Actions: from your Faction ability, Spellbooks, or Great Old One
Spellbook Requirement Actions: printed on Faction Card, one-time only
Unlimited Actions: performed before/after your main Action, any number of times

summary.title: Common Action: Recruit Cultist
bullets: Cost: 1 Power
Place 1 Acolyte from Pool into Area with any of your Units
If no Units on map, place anywhere
Cultists generate Power and Control Gates

summary.title: Common Action: Summon Monster
bullets: Cost: varies by Monster type (see Faction Card)
Place 1 Monster from Pool into Area where you Control a Gate
Limited by figures in your Pool

summary.title: Common Action: Awaken Great Old One
bullets: Cost: varies, multi-step process on Faction Card
Brings your Great Old One into play with its Special Ability
If Killed, must re-Awaken at full cost
Powerful but expensive; timing is critical

summary.title: Common Action: Create Gate
bullets: Cost: 3 Power, requires a Cultist in the Area, no existing Gate
Place Gate, immediately Control it with your Cultist
Only 1 Gate per Area ever
Gates provide Power (Gather Phase) and Doom (Doom Phase)

summary.title: Common Action: Move
bullets: Cost: 1 Power per Unit moved
Move any number of Units, each once, to adjacent Areas
Units can come from/go to different Areas in any combination

summary.title: Common Action: Battle
bullets: Cost: 1 Power, requires at least 1 Combat among your Units
Choose 1 enemy Faction in the Area to fight
1 battle per Battle Action, in 1 Area

summary.title: Common Action: Capture Cultist
bullets: Cost: 1 Power
Requires your Monster or Great Old One in same Area as enemy Cultist
Target must be unprotected (no friendly Monster or GOO guarding it)
Captured Cultist goes on your Faction Card; returned during Gather Power for 1 Power

summary.title: Unlimited Actions
bullets: Control/Abandon Gate: 0 Power, place/remove Cultist on Gate
Unlimited Battle: 1 Power, requires all 6 Spellbooks, 1 battle per Area per turn
Can be done before AND after your main Action each turn

summary.title: Battle Resolution: Pre-Battle
bullets: Attacker uses all Pre-Battle abilities first, then defender
Some abilities remove Units before dice roll
If all Units on one side are removed, battle ends immediately

summary.title: Battle Resolution: Rolling Dice
bullets: Each side rolls dice equal to their total Combat rating
6 = Kill, 4-5 = Pain, 1-3 = miss
Both sides roll simultaneously

summary.title: Battle Resolution: Assigning Results
bullets: Assign enemy's Kills to your Units first
Use Post-Battle abilities to modify results
Then assign enemy's Pains to your Units
Excess results are ignored

summary.title: Battle Resolution: Applying Results
bullets: Kills/Eliminations remove Units to Pool (apply first)
Pains retreat Units to adjacent Area without enemy Units from battle
Attacker applies Pains first, then defender
No valid retreat = Eliminate 1 Pained Unit, rest stay

summary.title: Spellbooks and How to Earn Them
bullets: 6 requirements on Faction Card; fulfill requirement, place any Spellbook
Spellbooks are permanent once earned
All 6 required to win and to unlock Unlimited Battle
Can earn multiple simultaneously if requirements met at same time

summary.title: Gather Power Phase
bullets: +1 Power per Cultist in play, +2 per Controlled Gate
+1 per Abandoned Gate on map (all players), +1 per Captured Cultist returned
Minimum Power Rule: your Power cannot be less than half the highest player's (rounded up)

summary.title: Determine First Player Phase
bullets: Most Power = new First Player (ties broken by previous First Player)
New First Player chooses clockwise or counterclockwise direction

summary.title: Doom Phase: Doom Track Advancement
bullets: All players advance Doom equal to their Controlled Gates (simultaneously)
Abandoned Gates give no Doom

summary.title: Doom Phase: Ritual of Annihilation
bullets: In turn order, each player may perform 1 Ritual
Cost: current Ritual Marker position (5-10 Power)
Gain: Doom equal to Controlled Gates + 1 Elder Sign per Great Old One in play
Ritual Marker advances after each Ritual (raising cost for next player)

summary.title: Instant Death and Elder Signs
bullets: Ritual Marker at Instant Death = game ends after this Doom Phase
Elder Signs: hidden tokens worth 1-3 Doom each, reveal at any time
Most players save Elder Signs until game end to conceal true score

summary.title: Victory Conditions
bullets: Game ends: 30+ Doom reached OR Instant Death triggered
Winner: highest Doom with all 6 Spellbooks
No 6 Spellbooks = eliminated from contention
Tie = shared victory; nobody has 6 Spellbooks = humanity wins

summary.title: Great Cthulhu Faction Overview
bullets: Units: 6 Acolytes, 4 Deep Ones, 2 Shoggoths, 2 Starspawn, Cthulhu (Combat 6)
Unique: Immortal (Elder Sign on death, cheap re-Awaken at 4 Power)
Key Spellbooks: Absorb, Devolve, Dreams, Regenerate, Submerge, Y'ha Nthlei
Playstyle: aggressive combat, strike and submerge, ruin enemy bases

summary.title: Crawling Chaos Faction Overview
bullets: Units: 6 Acolytes, 3 Nightgaunts, 2 Flying Polyps, 2 Hunting Horrors, Nyarlathotep
Unique: Flight (Nightgaunts/Hunting Horrors move anywhere for 1 Power)
Key Spellbooks: Abduct, Emissary, Invisibility, Madness, Seek and Destroy, Thousand Forms
Playstyle: opportunistic harassment, Power manipulation, hit-and-run

summary.title: Black Goat Faction Overview
bullets: Units: 6 Acolytes, 3 Ghouls, 2 Fungi, 4 Dark Young, Shub-Niggurath
Unique: Thousand Young (all Monsters cost 1 less Power)
Key Spellbooks: Avatar, Blood Sacrifice, Frenzy, Ghroth, Necrophagy, Red Sign
Playstyle: expansion, cheap units, remote disruption, Elder Sign farming

summary.title: Yellow Sign Faction Overview
bullets: Units: 6 Acolytes, 6 Undead, 2 Byakhee, King in Yellow, Hastur
Unique: Desecrate (place marker + summon Undead in specific Areas)
Key Spellbooks: He Who is Not to be Named, Passion, Screaming Dead, Shriek, Third Eye, Vengeance/Zingaya
Playstyle: nomadic disruption, double Actions, Desecration trail for Power and Elder Signs

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Apocalypse
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first cycle of Cthulhu Wars with four players, one per core faction. Player 1 is Great Cthulhu, Player 2 is Crawling Chaos, Player 3 is Black Goat, and Player 4 is Yellow Sign. We will cover the Action Phase and touch on the other phases. Follow along even if you have fewer players. Ready? The Old Ones stir.

walkthrough.title: Starting Positions
walkthrough.text: Everyone has 8 Power, a Controlled Gate, and 6 Acolytes in their Start Area. Player 1 (Great Cthulhu) is in the South Pacific. Player 2 (Crawling Chaos) is in the North Atlantic. Player 3 (Black Goat) is in Europe. Player 4 (Yellow Sign) is in Asia. Great Cthulhu is always the First Player, so Player 1 chooses the direction of play. Let's say clockwise.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Recruit a Cultist
walkthrough.text: Player 1 starts the Action Phase. You have 8 Power and all your Acolytes are already on the map. Wait, actually you start with all 6 Acolytes in the South Pacific. A solid first move is to Recruit? No, you already have all 6 out. Instead, Player 1 spends 1 Power to Move an Acolyte from the South Pacific to an adjacent Area. This costs 1 Power, leaving you at 7. Spreading out early is important for setting up future Gates.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 (Crawling Chaos): Move and Spread
walkthrough.text: Player 2 spends 2 Power to Move two Acolytes out of the North Atlantic to adjacent Areas. One heads toward North America, the other toward West Africa. This costs 2 Power, leaving Player 2 at 6. Crawling Chaos wants to spread out quickly to set up secondary Gates and fulfill Spellbook requirements.

walkthrough.title: Player 3 (Black Goat): Create a Gate
walkthrough.text: Player 3 decides to keep their Acolytes close to Europe for now. Instead, they spend 1 Power to Move one Acolyte to an adjacent land Area, planning to Create a Gate there next turn. This costs 1 Power, leaving Player 3 at 7. Black Goat wants to build a second Gate quickly to maximize Power income.

walkthrough.title: Player 4 (Yellow Sign): Awaken the King in Yellow
walkthrough.text: Player 4 wastes no time. The King in Yellow is cheap to Awaken, costing only 4 Power. Player 4 follows the steps on their Faction Card to Awaken the King in Asia. This leaves them at 4 Power. The King in Yellow is the cheapest Great Old One to bring out and is central to Yellow Sign's strategy of spreading Desecration across the map.

walkthrough.title: Why Awakening Early Was Smart
walkthrough.text: Yellow Sign's strength comes from the King in Yellow moving around the map, Desecrating Areas, and spawning free Undead. Bringing him out in the very first Action Phase gives Player 4 maximum time to spread Desecration markers. The King only costs 4 Power to Awaken and can be rebuilt cheaply if Killed. Other factions should generally wait a bit longer before Awakening their more expensive Great Old Ones.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Create a Gate
walkthrough.text: On Player 1's second turn, they spend 3 Power to Create a Gate in the Area they moved an Acolyte to. The Acolyte immediately Controls it. Now Player 1 has two Controlled Gates, which will generate more Power and Doom. Power drops from 7 to 4. Building Gates is always a priority in the early game.

walkthrough.title: Player 4's Mistake: Ignoring Gate Building
walkthrough.text: Player 4 is excited about the King in Yellow and spends their next 4 Power moving the King and an Acolyte across the map to Desecrate Africa. This looks aggressive, but Player 4 has completely ignored building a second Gate. With only one Gate, Player 4 will have poor Power income next round and low Doom generation. Meanwhile, Players 1, 2, and 3 are all building their second Gates.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Gates Before Glory
walkthrough.text: The lesson here is critical: Gates are the engine of Cthulhu Wars. Each Controlled Gate gives you 2 Power during the Gather Power Phase and 1 Doom during the Doom Phase. A player with one Gate earning 2 Power per cycle falls behind a player with three Gates earning 6 Power. No matter how tempting it is to go on the offensive early, always build at least a second Gate first. Gates fund everything else.

walkthrough.title: Player 3 (Black Goat): Summon a Monster
walkthrough.text: Player 3 has moved an Acolyte out and created a second Gate. Now they use their Thousand Young ability to Summon a Ghoul for free (cost 1 minus 1 from Thousand Young equals 0). This is incredible efficiency. The Ghoul protects the Cultist from Capture and can be used with Necrophagy later. Black Goat's cheap Monsters are one of their greatest strengths.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 (Crawling Chaos): Summon a Nightgaunt
walkthrough.text: Player 2 spends 1 Power to Summon a Nightgaunt at their Gate. Nightgaunts have 0 Combat, but they protect Cultists from Capture and their Abduct ability (once that Spellbook is earned) will let them Eliminate enemy Cultists in Pre-Battle. Even with 0 Combat, cheap Monsters are incredibly useful for protection.

walkthrough.title: Player 4's Mistake: Leaving Gates Undefended
walkthrough.text: Player 4 moved the King in Yellow and most Acolytes away from Asia, leaving only a single Acolyte on the home Gate with no Monster protection. Player 2 (Crawling Chaos) notices this. On their next turn, Player 2 uses the Flight ability to send a Nightgaunt directly to Asia, and then Captures Player 4's undefended Cultist. Player 4 loses control of their only Gate.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Never Leave a Cultist Alone on a Gate
walkthrough.text: Always leave a Monster or your Great Old One to protect Cultists on Gates. A lone Cultist on a Gate is an easy Capture target for any opponent with a Monster or Great Old One in an adjacent Area. Losing your Gate means losing 2 Power per cycle and 1 Doom per Doom Phase. The Capture also gives your opponent 1 Power. Always have at least one Monster bodyguard with each Gate Cultist.

walkthrough.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Summon a Deep One
walkthrough.text: Player 1 spends 1 Power to Summon a Deep One at one of their Gates. Deep Ones cost 1 Power and have 1 Combat. They protect Cultists, contribute to battles, and can later be used with the Absorb Spellbook to supercharge a Shoggoth. Always have some units protecting your Gates.

walkthrough.title: Continue the Action Phase
walkthrough.text: Play continues around the table. Players keep spending Power on Actions until everyone hits 0. In a typical first Action Phase, players build 1-2 new Gates, Summon a couple of Monsters, and spread out a bit. Battles are relatively rare in the first cycle. Focus on infrastructure, not destruction.

walkthrough.title: Gather Power Phase
walkthrough.text: The Action Phase ends. Time to Gather Power. Player 1 (Cthulhu) has 5 Cultists in play and 2 Controlled Gates, earning 5 plus 4 equals 9 Power. Player 3 (Black Goat) has similar numbers. Player 4 (Yellow Sign) lost their Gate, so they only have Cultists earning Power, getting maybe 5 Power total. The Minimum Power Rule helps, but Player 4 is now behind.

walkthrough.title: Determine First Player and Doom Phase
walkthrough.text: The player with the most Power becomes First Player. Then in the Doom Phase, each player gains Doom equal to their Controlled Gates. Players with 2-3 Gates gain 2-3 Doom. Player 4 with 0 Gates gains nothing. No one performs a Ritual this early because the Power cost of 5 is too steep when you need every point for the next Action Phase. The cycle repeats.

walkthrough.title: Player 4's Redemption
walkthrough.text: Player 4 has learned their lessons. In the second Action Phase, they immediately Recruit an Acolyte in Asia, Create a new Gate there, and leave a Monster to protect it before sending the King in Yellow on his Desecration tour. Smart play. Player 4 is back in the game with a secure Power base and can now focus on Spellbook requirements.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen the core actions in play, watched Player 4 learn from two critical mistakes, and experienced a full cycle of all four phases. Remember: build Gates before going on the offensive, always protect your Cultists with Monsters, and focus on earning Spellbooks in the early game. The Great Old Ones are patient. Build your power base first.

walkthrough.title: Before the Stars Align
walkthrough.text:
Before you continue your game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for faction-specific beginner advice and early versus late game strategy.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every Spellbook, Monster, Great Old One, and game term.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. May the Old Ones favor you!

summary.title: Welcome to the Apocalypse
bullets: 4-player scripted tutorial: Great Cthulhu, Crawling Chaos, Black Goat, Yellow Sign
Covers a full first cycle through all four phases
Follow along even with fewer players

summary.title: Starting Positions
bullets: All players: 8 Power, 1 Controlled Gate, 6 Acolytes in Start Area
Great Cthulhu is always First Player
First Player chooses clockwise or counterclockwise

summary.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Recruit a Cultist
bullets: Player 1 Moves an Acolyte to an adjacent Area (1 Power)
Spreading Cultists early sets up future Gate locations
All 6 Acolytes start in play already

summary.title: Player 2 (Crawling Chaos): Move and Spread
bullets: Moves 2 Acolytes to different adjacent Areas (2 Power)
Crawling Chaos wants to spread for Spellbook requirements
Setting up future Gate locations in multiple regions

summary.title: Player 3 (Black Goat): Create a Gate
bullets: Moves 1 Acolyte to adjacent Area (1 Power)
Planning to Create a Gate there next turn
Black Goat prioritizes a second Gate for Power income

summary.title: Player 4 (Yellow Sign): Awaken the King in Yellow
bullets: Awakens King in Yellow in Asia (4 Power)
Cheapest Great Old One to bring out
Central to Yellow Sign's Desecration strategy

summary.title: Why Awakening Early Was Smart
bullets: King in Yellow enables Desecration across the map
Cheap to Awaken (4 Power) and cheap to rebuild if Killed
More expensive Great Old Ones (Cthulhu, Nyarlathotep) should wait

summary.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Create a Gate
bullets: Creates a Gate in the Area with a moved Acolyte (3 Power)
Now has 2 Controlled Gates for double Power/Doom income
Building Gates is the top early-game priority

summary.title: Player 4's Mistake: Ignoring Gate Building
bullets: Player 4 spends all remaining Power on moving and Desecrating
Still has only 1 Gate while other players have 2+
Will have poor Power income and low Doom generation

summary.title: Lesson: Gates Before Glory
bullets: Each Controlled Gate = +2 Power (Gather Phase) + 1 Doom (Doom Phase)
Always build at least a second Gate before going offensive
Gates fund everything: Monsters, Great Old Ones, Rituals

summary.title: Player 3 (Black Goat): Summon a Monster
bullets: Summons a Ghoul for free (Thousand Young reduces cost to 0)
Ghoul protects Cultist from Capture
Black Goat's cheap Monsters are a key strength

summary.title: Player 2 (Crawling Chaos): Summon a Nightgaunt
bullets: Summons Nightgaunt at Gate (1 Power)
0 Combat but protects Cultists from Capture
Abduct Spellbook later makes Nightgaunts terrifying

summary.title: Player 4's Mistake: Leaving Gates Undefended
bullets: Player 4 left only 1 Acolyte on home Gate with no Monster guard
Crawling Chaos sends Nightgaunt via Flight and Captures the Cultist
Player 4 loses control of their only Gate

summary.title: Lesson: Never Leave a Cultist Alone on a Gate
bullets: Always have a Monster or Great Old One guarding Gate Cultists
Lone Cultists are easy Capture targets
Losing a Gate costs 2 Power/cycle and 1 Doom/Phase

summary.title: Player 1 (Cthulhu): Summon a Deep One
bullets: Summons Deep One at Gate (1 Power, 1 Combat)
Protects Cultist and contributes to battles
Later pairs with Absorb Spellbook for powerful combos

summary.title: Continue the Action Phase
bullets: Players keep taking Actions until all at 0 Power
First cycle: build 1-2 Gates, Summon a few Monsters, spread out
Battles rare in the first cycle; focus on infrastructure

summary.title: Gather Power Phase
bullets: All players earn Power simultaneously
+1 per Cultist in play, +2 per Controlled Gate, +1 per Abandoned Gate
Player 4 with 0 Gates earns much less; Minimum Power Rule helps slightly

summary.title: Determine First Player and Doom Phase
bullets: Most Power = new First Player, chooses play direction
Doom Phase: gain Doom equal to Controlled Gates
Rituals too expensive this early; save Power for next Action Phase

summary.title: Player 4's Redemption
bullets: Recruits Acolyte, Creates Gate, leaves Monster protection
Then sends King in Yellow out on Desecration tour
Secure Power base before offensive operations

summary.title: Congratulations
bullets: You have seen all core Actions and a full phase cycle
Player 4 learned: build Gates first, protect Cultists with Monsters
Early game: infrastructure and Spellbooks over combat

summary.title: Before the Stars Align
bullets: Check General Tips for faction-specific beginner advice
Reference the Appendix for Spellbooks, Monsters, and game terms
Ask your personal GM Guide via Q&A tab for help anytime during play

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before diving into strategy, here are the biggest mistakes new players make. First, neglecting Gate building in the early game. Gates are your engine for both Power and Doom, and every turn you delay building one costs you resources across the entire game. Second, leaving Cultists unprotected on Gates. A lone Cultist is a Capture waiting to happen. Third, Awakening your Great Old One too early when you cannot protect it. A Killed Great Old One costs you the re-Awaken price plus a whole Action Phase of vulnerability. Fourth, ignoring Spellbook requirements. You cannot win without all six, and they grant you game-changing abilities. Fifth, spending all your Power on Rituals and entering the next Action Phase broke. Balance aggression with sustainability.

walkthrough.title: Early Game: Build Your Power Base
walkthrough.text: In the first two to three Action Phases, focus on acquiring Spellbooks, building Gates, and Summoning a minimal army. Be selective about when to Awaken your Great Old One because the cost generally leaves you weak for the rest of that Action Phase. Battles in the early game should be opportunistic, aimed at taking Control of enemy Gates or fulfilling Spellbook requirements, not waging total war. Two to three Controlled Gates should be your baseline before you start thinking about offense.

walkthrough.title: Late Game: Rituals and Reduction
walkthrough.text: The late game starts once you have your Great Old One out and three to four Spellbooks. You can now afford an occasional Ritual of Annihilation. Remember that earlier Rituals are cheaper (the marker starts at 5 and only goes up), so performing Rituals before your opponents makes theirs more expensive. The purpose of Battle shifts in the late game: you now fight to reduce enemies' Gates so they score fewer Doom points on Rituals. If one player surges ahead, rally the others against them. If you are the one surging, try to break up those alliances.

walkthrough.title: Great Cthulhu: Beginner Advice
walkthrough.text: Your faction is focused on destruction. Get Cthulhu out by Action Phase two or three, even if he is vulnerable to attack. You can re-Awaken him cheaply at 4 Power and gain an Elder Sign each time he dies. Focus on earning your Kill and Devour Spellbook requirements early, as they come naturally through combat. In the late game, use Submerge to strike critical enemy Areas from the ocean, and Regenerate or Absorb to make your army nearly indestructible in battle. Devolve keeps your Cultists safe from Capture by replacing them with Deep Ones reactively.

walkthrough.title: Crawling Chaos: Beginner Advice
walkthrough.text: Focus on safety and Power-building in the early game. Use Flight to set up bases in distant Areas that are hard for opponents to reach. Nightgaunts with Abduct discourage enemies from attacking your Cultists. Do not bring out Nyarlathotep early because he is stronger later when he has more Spellbooks (his Combat equals enemy factions in his Area times your Spellbooks). Your most awkward Spellbook requirement is often Capture a Cultist, so watch for any opportunity. In the late game, Nyarlathotep becomes terrifying with up to twelve dice, and Thousand Forms lets you steal Power to fuel Rituals.

walkthrough.title: Black Goat: Beginner Advice
walkthrough.text: The most common novice mistake is spreading Cultists everywhere to immediately get the 'Have Units in X Areas' Spellbook requirements. Your enemies will pounce once you are out of Power. Build up at a steady pace instead. Use cheap Monsters (free with Thousand Young) to occupy Areas for Spellbook purposes rather than vulnerable Cultists. Create a stronghold where you can safely Summon Shub-Niggurath. In the late game, Necrophagy and Avatar let you strike at distant targets, while Blood Sacrifice generates a steady flow of Elder Signs.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Sign: Beginner Advice
walkthrough.text: You earn Spellbooks more slowly than other factions, so focus on them. Awaken the King in Yellow immediately in the first Action Phase. The King costs only 4 Power, moves quickly, and enables Desecration. Leave a trail of Monster breadcrumbs behind the King as you move across the map. These Undead generate Power from Desecration tokens. Do not worry about having few Gates because Third Eye and your two Great Old Ones generate Elder Signs instead. Screaming Dead is one of the most powerful Spellbooks in the game because it gives you double Actions.

walkthrough.title: Power Management is Everything
walkthrough.text: Power is the lifeblood of Cthulhu Wars. Every Action costs Power, and you only get it back once per cycle during the Gather Power Phase. Being the last player with Power in an Action Phase is incredibly strong because you get to take multiple Actions unopposed. Conversely, running out first means watching helplessly as opponents mess with your stuff. Always calculate your Power budget. Know how much you need for essential Actions and how much you can afford to spend on Rituals. The Minimum Power Rule prevents total devastation, but half the leader's Power is still a weak position.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't neglect Gate building in the early game — Gates fund everything
Never leave a Cultist alone on a Gate without Monster protection
Don't Awaken your Great Old One before you can protect it
Don't ignore Spellbook requirements — all 6 required to win
Don't spend all Power on Rituals and enter next phase broke

summary.title: Early Game: Build Your Power Base
bullets: First 2-3 Action Phases: Gates, Spellbooks, minimal army
Battles should be opportunistic, not all-out war
Aim for 2-3 Controlled Gates before going offensive
Be selective about Great Old One timing

summary.title: Late Game: Rituals and Reduction
bullets: Late game starts with Great Old One out + 3-4 Spellbooks
Perform Rituals early (cost starts at 5, escalates to 10)
Battle to reduce enemy Gates and Doom scoring
Rally allies against the leader, or break alliances if leading

summary.title: Great Cthulhu: Beginner Advice
bullets: Awaken Cthulhu by Action Phase 2-3, even if vulnerable
Cheap re-Awaken (4 Power) + Elder Sign on death = low risk
Devolve protects Cultists; Submerge enables surprise strikes
Focus on combat to earn Kill/Devour Spellbook requirements

summary.title: Crawling Chaos: Beginner Advice
bullets: Early game: safety and Power-building via Flight
Delay Nyarlathotep — he scales with Spellbook count
Abduct with Nightgaunts deters attacks on your Cultists
Late game: Nyarlathotep up to 12 dice, Thousand Forms for Power

summary.title: Black Goat: Beginner Advice
bullets: Don't rush Cultist diaspora for Spellbooks — enemies will pounce
Use free Monsters (Thousand Young) to occupy Areas instead
Build a safe stronghold for Shub-Niggurath
Late game: Necrophagy + Avatar for distant strikes, Blood Sacrifice for Elder Signs

summary.title: Yellow Sign: Beginner Advice
bullets: Awaken King in Yellow immediately (first Action Phase, 4 Power)
Leave Undead breadcrumbs for Power from Desecration tokens
Screaming Dead gives double Actions — get it early
Third Eye + both Great Old Ones = steady Elder Sign income

summary.title: Power Management is Everything
bullets: Being last player with Power = multiple unopposed Actions
Running out first = watching helplessly
Budget Power for essentials before spending on Rituals
Minimum Power Rule prevents total devastation but half-Power is still weak

walkthrough.title: Gate Economy Optimization
walkthrough.text: The difference between two and four Controlled Gates is staggering. Two Gates give you 4 Power and 2 Doom per cycle. Four Gates give you 8 Power and 4 Doom. With a Ritual of Annihilation on top of that, four Gates yields 8 Doom per Doom Phase plus Elder Signs. In the early game, invest heavily in Gates even at the expense of combat readiness. Three Gates by the end of the second Action Phase puts you on a strong trajectory. Four by the third is dominant. Every Power point spent on a Gate pays for itself within two cycles.

walkthrough.title: Ritual Timing and the Annihilation Arms Race
walkthrough.text: The Ritual of Annihilation track is one of the most important strategic elements. The first Ritual costs only 5 Power. Each subsequent one costs more. Being the first to Ritual is advantageous because you pay less and drive the cost up for everyone else. However, spending 5-10 Power on a Ritual means entering the next Action Phase significantly weaker. The key decision is whether the Doom and Elder Signs gained outweigh the Power disadvantage. With three or more Gates, the answer is almost always yes. With fewer Gates, it is often better to skip the Ritual and invest that Power in expanding.

walkthrough.title: Great Cthulhu: Advanced Combat Tactics
walkthrough.text: Cthulhu's endgame involves the Submerge plus Strike cycle. Move your army to an ocean Area, then Submerge when threatened. On the next turn, surface at any ocean Area and attack a coastal enemy. With Absorb, a Shoggoth eating a Deep One and a Cultist rolls 5 extra dice on top of its base 2. Combined with Cthulhu's Devour (a free Pre-Battle Elimination), you remove an enemy unit before dice are even rolled, then roll 13 or more dice. Regenerate on Starspawn converts their Kills to Pains, meaning your army barely takes losses. The cycle repeats: strike, Submerge, strike again elsewhere.

walkthrough.title: Crawling Chaos: The Harbinger Power Engine
walkthrough.text: Nyarlathotep's Harbinger ability is the key to Crawling Chaos's late game. Whenever an enemy Great Old One is Pained in a battle where Nyarlathotep is present, you gain 2 Elder Signs or 2 Power. Combined with Madness (you choose where all Pained Units retreat), you can direct the Pained Great Old One to an inconvenient Area while pocketing Elder Signs. Use He Who is Not to be Named from Yellow Sign, or ally with Yellow Sign's Vengeance to guarantee enemy GOO kills. Thousand Forms steals Power from opponents, letting you fund Rituals while keeping enemies weak.

walkthrough.title: Black Goat: The Fortress Strategy
walkthrough.text: Black Goat's optimal strategy involves building an impenetrable fortress in two to three adjacent Areas and using remote abilities to disrupt opponents. With Red Sign, Dark Young Control Gates (immune to Capture). With Frenzy, your Cultists have Combat 1, making your fortress surprisingly well-defended. Blood Sacrifice converts Cultists into Elder Signs during every Doom Phase. Avatar teleports Shub-Niggurath to distant Areas to steal Gates, and Necrophagy sends Ghouls into any battle on the map to add Pain results. You do not need to leave home to win.

walkthrough.title: Yellow Sign: The Desecration Engine
walkthrough.text: Yellow Sign's path to victory is unique. You typically control fewer Gates than other factions but compensate with Third Eye, which earns an Elder Sign every time you Desecrate while both Great Old Ones are in play. The Desecration engine works like this: Move the King to an un-Desecrated Area (1 Power), Desecrate (1 Power), gain a free Undead, use Screaming Dead for a second Action. With Third Eye, each Desecration is also an Elder Sign for just 2 Power total. Six Desecrations can yield 6 Elder Signs worth 6-18 Doom in hidden value. Zingaya supplements this by Eliminating an enemy Cultist and replacing it with an Undead.

walkthrough.title: Kingmaker Politics and Alliance Dynamics
walkthrough.text: Cthulhu Wars is inherently political. If one player surges ahead in Doom, the others must coordinate to stop them. The key is recognizing the leader early and acting before the gap becomes insurmountable. Crawling Chaos is often the natural peacekeeper because Madness and Thousand Forms make them dangerous to anger. Black Goat can punish anyone with Ghroth regardless of distance. Great Cthulhu is the blunt instrument, smashing Gates with overwhelming force. Yellow Sign disrupts everyone equally. Promises are not binding, so always see the benefit before acting on someone else's behalf.

walkthrough.title: Spellbook Order Optimization
walkthrough.text: The order in which you earn Spellbooks dramatically affects your game. Each Faction has an optimal early Spellbook that accelerates everything else. For Great Cthulhu, Devolve is often first because it immediately protects your Cultists. For Crawling Chaos, Seek and Destroy provides early defense and offense. For Black Goat, Frenzy gives your Cultists Combat, making them much harder to bully. For Yellow Sign, Screaming Dead is the highest-priority Spellbook after Awakening the King because double Actions are incredibly powerful. Plan your first three Spellbooks before the game starts and engineer your Actions to earn them as fast as possible.

walkthrough.title: Elder Sign Gambling and Hidden Information
walkthrough.text: Elder Signs introduce hidden information that creates bluffing opportunities. There are 18 tokens worth 1, 12 worth 2, and 6 worth 3. The expected value of a random Elder Sign is approximately 1.5 Doom. Players who earn many Elder Signs have a hidden Doom reserve that can swing the final score by 5-15 points. The decision of when to reveal Elder Signs is crucial. Revealing early lets you use the Doom immediately but alerts opponents to your true position. Saving them conceals your strength but risks the game ending before you can use them. Most experienced players reveal only when forced or to trigger the 30 Doom game-end at the optimal moment.

walkthrough.title: The Endgame Sprint: Triggering Victory
walkthrough.text: The endgame of Cthulhu Wars is a sprint. Once any player nears 30 Doom, the game can end at any moment. The ideal play is to trigger the end on your terms, when you have the most Doom and all 6 Spellbooks, and ideally when an opponent is close but lacks their sixth Spellbook (making them ineligible to win). Watch the Doom Track constantly. Calculate how many points each Ritual will generate for each player. Sometimes the winning move is to NOT perform a Ritual, denying the game-end trigger while you quietly accumulate Elder Signs. Other times, you want to rush Instant Death before a trailing opponent completes their Spellbooks.

summary.title: Gate Economy Optimization
bullets: 2 Gates = 4 Power + 2 Doom/cycle; 4 Gates = 8 Power + 4 Doom/cycle
With Ritual: 4 Gates = 8 Doom/Phase + Elder Signs
Invest heavily in Gates early, even at expense of combat readiness
Aim for 3 Gates by end of second Action Phase

summary.title: Ritual Timing and the Annihilation Arms Race
bullets: First Ritual costs 5 Power; each subsequent one costs more
Being first to Ritual: pay less, drive cost up for opponents
3+ Gates = Ritual almost always worth it
Fewer Gates = often better to skip and invest in expansion

summary.title: Great Cthulhu: Advanced Combat Tactics
bullets: Submerge-Strike cycle: surface at any ocean, attack, Submerge again
Absorb: Shoggoth eats allies for +Combat dice (can roll 7+ dice)
Devour: free Pre-Battle Elimination before dice roll
Regenerate: Starspawn convert Kills to Pains, army barely takes losses

summary.title: Crawling Chaos: The Harbinger Power Engine
bullets: Harbinger: 2 Elder Signs or 2 Power when enemy GOO is Pained near Nyarlathotep
Madness: choose where ALL Pained Units retreat after any battle
Thousand Forms: steal Power from opponents to fund Rituals
Late-game Nyarlathotep can roll up to 12 Combat dice

summary.title: Black Goat: The Fortress Strategy
bullets: Build impenetrable 2-3 Area fortress, use remote abilities
Red Sign: Dark Young Control Gates (immune to Capture)
Blood Sacrifice: Elder Sign every Doom Phase
Avatar + Necrophagy: remote Gate stealing without leaving home

summary.title: Yellow Sign: The Desecration Engine
bullets: Third Eye: Elder Sign per Desecration with both GOOs in play
Desecrate cost: 2 Power total (1 Move + 1 Desecrate) = 1 Elder Sign
6 Desecrations = 6 Elder Signs worth 6-18 hidden Doom
Screaming Dead doubles Actions per turn for maximum efficiency

summary.title: Kingmaker Politics and Alliance Dynamics
bullets: Identify and target the Doom leader early before gap widens
Crawling Chaos: natural peacekeeper (Madness/Thousand Forms)
Black Goat: punishes anyone at distance (Ghroth)
Promises are not binding — see the benefit before acting

summary.title: Spellbook Order Optimization
bullets: Cthulhu first Spellbook: Devolve (immediate Cultist protection)
Crawling Chaos: Seek and Destroy (early defense + offense)
Black Goat: Frenzy (Cultists gain Combat 1)
Yellow Sign: Screaming Dead (double Actions, game-changing)

summary.title: Elder Sign Gambling and Hidden Information
bullets: Expected value per Elder Sign: ~1.5 Doom (tokens worth 1, 2, or 3)
Many Elder Signs = 5-15 hidden Doom reserve
Reveal early to use Doom but alert opponents
Save to conceal strength, reveal at end for surprise victory

summary.title: The Endgame Sprint: Triggering Victory
bullets: Game ends at 30+ Doom or Instant Death
Trigger game end when you lead AND opponent lacks 6th Spellbook
Sometimes NOT performing a Ritual is the winning move
Calculate Ritual Doom for all players before deciding

entries.definition: The first of four phases in each game cycle. Players take turns performing Actions by spending Power. Each turn you take exactly one Common, Unique, or Spellbook Requirement Action, plus any number of Unlimited Actions. The phase ends when all players are at 0 Power.

The second phase. All players simultaneously earn Power: +1 per Cultist in play, +2 per Controlled Gate, +1 per Abandoned Gate on the map (everyone), +1 per Captured Cultist returned. The Minimum Power Rule ensures no player has less than half the leader's Power (rounded up).

The third phase. The player with the most Power becomes First Player and chooses the direction of play (clockwise or counterclockwise). Ties are broken by the previous First Player choosing among the tied players.
The fourth phase. All players advance Doom equal to Controlled Gates, then in turn order may perform a Ritual of Annihilation. Some special abilities and Spellbook requirements also trigger here.

The resource spent to perform Actions during the Action Phase. Earned during the Gather Power Phase from Cultists, Gates, Abandoned Gates, and Captured Cultists. Tracked on the Power Track on your Faction Card (max 20, can wrap around).

Victory points tracked on the Doom Track. Earned from Controlled Gates during the Doom Phase, Rituals of Annihilation, and Elder Sign tokens. The player with the most Doom and all 6 Spellbooks wins. Game ends at 30+ Doom or Instant Death.

A structure placed in an Area, limited to one per Area. A Gate is Controlled when a Cultist (or Dark Young with Red Sign) is on top of it, and Abandoned when no Cultist is on it. Controlled Gates provide 2 Power during Gather Power and 1 Doom during the Doom Phase.
A Gate with a Cultist on top of it. Only one Cultist can Control a Gate at a time. Controlled Gates provide 2 Power during Gather Power Phase and Doom during the Doom Phase.
A Gate with no Cultist on top. All players gain 1 Power per Abandoned Gate during Gather Power Phase. Any player with a Cultist in the Area can Control it as an Unlimited Action.
One of six special ability cards per Faction. Earned by fulfilling requirements on the Faction Card. Permanent once earned. All 6 required to be eligible to win. Also unlocks Unlimited Battle.

Hidden token drawn from a cloth bag, worth 1, 2, or 3 Doom points. Earned primarily from Rituals of Annihilation (1 per Great Old One in play) and certain abilities. Can be revealed at any time to add to your Doom total. 36 total tokens (18x1, 12x2, 6x3).
Your player board containing: Faction Name, Power Track, Unique Ability, Setup Info, Unit stats (Cost and Combat), Great Old One info, and 6 Spellbook Requirement slots.

The collection of your not-yet-in-play Spellbooks, figures, and tokens kept near your Faction Card. Units in your Pool are not in play. You are limited by the number of figures in your Pool for each Unit type.

An optional action during the Doom Phase. Costs Power equal to the Ritual Marker position (5-10). Advances the Ritual Marker, gains Doom equal to Controlled Gates, and earns 1 Elder Sign per Great Old One in play. When the marker reaches Instant Death, the game ends.

The final space on the Ritual of Annihilation track. When reached, the game ends at the conclusion of that Doom Phase. All remaining players may still perform their Ritual. All players reveal Elder Signs before declaring victory.
After the Gather Power Phase, if your Power is less than half the highest player's Power, increase it to half that amount (rounded up). Prevents total snowballing.

The basic unit type shared by all factions. Cost: 1 Power to Recruit. Combat: 0. Each faction has exactly 6 Acolytes. Cultists produce 1 Power each during Gather Power Phase and are the only units that can Control Gates (except Dark Young with Red Sign).

Faction-specific combat units with varying costs and Combat ratings. Summoned at Controlled Gates. Protect Cultists from Capture and provide Combat dice in battle. Each faction has unique Monster types.

The most powerful unit for each faction. Awakened through a multi-step process unique to each faction. Provides a Special Ability while in play. If Killed, returns to Pool and must be re-Awakened. Protects Cultists from Capture by enemy Monsters.
A die result of 6 during battle. The targeted unit is removed from the map and returned to its owner's Pool. Kills are always assigned before Pains.

A die result of 4 or 5 during battle. The targeted unit must retreat to an adjacent Area that does not contain units from the enemy you just battled. If no valid retreat exists, one Pained unit is Eliminated instead.

Removal of a unit from the map to its owner's Pool, caused by abilities (not dice rolls). Unlike Kills, Eliminations are caused by Pre-Battle abilities, Post-Battle abilities, or special Actions. Some Post-Battle abilities distinguish between Kills and Eliminations.

Abilities that trigger before dice are rolled in battle. The attacker uses all Pre-Battle abilities first, then the defender. Can remove units before combat begins. If all units on one side are removed, the battle ends immediately.

Abilities that trigger after dice are rolled, during result assignment. Can modify, add, or redirect Kill and Pain results. Both sides can use all their Post-Battle abilities regardless of results assigned to them. Some work even when not involved in the battle.

The number of dice a unit contributes to battle, listed on the Faction Card. Ranges from 0 (most Cultists, Nightgaunts, Undead) to 6+ (Cthulhu, late-game Nyarlathotep). Some units have variable Combat based on conditions.

A Common Action costing 1 Power. Requires your Monster or Great Old One in the same Area as an unprotected enemy Cultist. The Captured Cultist goes on your Faction Card and is returned during Gather Power Phase, granting you 1 Power.
A Common Action costing 1 Power. Place 1 Acolyte from your Pool into an Area with any of your Units. If you have no Units on the map, place anywhere. Only 1 per action.
A Common Action. Cost varies by Monster type. Place 1 Monster from your Pool into an Area where you Control a Gate. Only 1 per action.
A Common Action with faction-specific multi-step requirements and costs. Brings your Great Old One from your Pool onto the map. Its Special Ability becomes available while in play. Only 1 per action.
A Common Action costing 3 Power. Requires a Cultist in the Area and no existing Gate. Place a Gate and immediately Control it. Only 1 Gate per Area, only 1 per action.
A Common Action. Cost: 1 Power per Unit moved. Move any number of units to adjacent Areas. Each unit moves once. Can move units from/to different Areas freely.
A Common Action costing 1 Power. Initiate combat in an Area where you and an enemy both have units. Requires at least 1 Combat. Choose one enemy faction to fight. One battle per action, one Area.

An Unlimited Action costing 0 Power. Place a Cultist onto an Abandoned Gate to Control it, or remove a Cultist from a Gate to Abandon it. Can be done any number of times on your turn, before or after your main Action.
An Unlimited Action costing 1 Power. Requires all 6 Spellbooks. Allows additional battles before or after your main Action. Still limited to 1 battle per Area per turn.

Great Old One of the Great Cthulhu faction. Cost: 10 Power to Awaken (4 to re-Awaken via Immortal). Combat: 6. Special Ability: Devour (Pre-Battle, Eliminate an enemy Cultist or Monster). Starts in R'lyeh (South Pacific).

Great Old One of the Crawling Chaos faction. Cost: 10 Power to Awaken. Combat: number of enemy factions with units in his Area multiplied by your Spellbook count. Special Ability: Harbinger (gain 2 Power or 2 Elder Signs when an enemy Great Old One is Pained in his battle).

Great Old One of the Black Goat faction. Cost: 8 Power to Awaken. Combat: varies. Provides the foundation for Black Goat's fortress strategy. Used with Avatar Spellbook to teleport and disrupt opponents.

Great Old One of the Yellow Sign faction. Cost: 4 Power to Awaken. Combat: 0. The cheapest Great Old One. Enables Desecration and spreads across the map rapidly. Works with Screaming Dead for double Actions.

Second Great Old One of the Yellow Sign faction. Cost: 10 Power to Awaken. Combat: varies. Special Ability: Vengeance (Hastur assigns enemy Kills to enemy Units rather than the enemy choosing). Paired with He Who is Not to be Named for assassination strikes.
Monster belonging to the Great Cthulhu faction. Cost: 1 Power. Combat: 1. Count: 4 in Pool. Used as frontline fighters, Gate protectors, and fodder for Shoggoth's Absorb ability.

Monster belonging to the Great Cthulhu faction. Cost: 2 Power. Combat: 2 (greatly increased with Absorb Spellbook). Count: 2 in Pool. Absorb lets the Shoggoth consume allied units to add their Combat to its own during battle.

Monster belonging to the Great Cthulhu faction. Cost: 3 Power. Combat: 3 (up to 6 with Spellbooks). Count: 2 in Pool. With Regenerate Spellbook, Starspawn convert Kills to Pains, making them extremely durable.

Monster belonging to the Crawling Chaos faction. Cost: 1 Power. Combat: 0. Count: 3 in Pool. Despite 0 Combat, protects Cultists from Capture. With Abduct Spellbook, Eliminates an enemy Cultist in Pre-Battle. Can use Flight to move anywhere.

Monster belonging to the Crawling Chaos faction. Cost: 2 Power. Combat: 1. Count: 2 in Pool. With Invisibility Spellbook, a Flying Polyp can be exempted from all battle results, making it invulnerable for that battle.

Monster belonging to the Crawling Chaos faction. Cost: 3 Power. Combat: 2. Count: 2 in Pool. Can use Flight to move anywhere for 1 Power. With Seek and Destroy, joins any battle for free from anywhere on the map.
Monster belonging to the Black Goat faction. Cost: 1 Power (0 with Thousand Young). Combat: 0. Count: 3 in Pool. Used with Necrophagy to teleport into any battle on the map and add Pain results.
Monster belonging to the Black Goat faction. Cost: 2 Power (1 with Thousand Young). Combat: 1. Count: 2 in Pool. Versatile unit for area control and moderate combat contribution.

Monster belonging to the Black Goat faction. Cost: 3 Power (2 with Thousand Young). Combat: 2. Count: 4 in Pool. With Red Sign Spellbook, Dark Young can Control Gates instead of Cultists, making them immune to Capture.

Monster belonging to the Yellow Sign faction. Cost: 1 Power (often free via Desecration). Combat: 0. Count: 6 in Pool. Serve as bodyguards for the King in Yellow. Generated when Desecrating Areas and via Zingaya.

Monster belonging to the Yellow Sign faction. Cost: 1 Power. Combat: special (1 more die than the number of Byakhee in the Area). Count: 2 in Pool. Summoned via Shriek of the Byakhee Spellbook to bolster Great Old Ones in battle.
When Cthulhu is Killed, immediately gain 1 Elder Sign. Cthulhu can then be re-Awakened for only 4 Power instead of the full 10. This makes Cthulhu the most resilient Great Old One in the game.
Nightgaunts and Hunting Horrors can Move to any Area on the map for 1 Power each, rather than only adjacent Areas. This gives Crawling Chaos unmatched mobility for hit-and-run tactics.

entries.term: The Thousand Young (Black Goat Faction Ability)
entries.definition: All of Black Goat's Monsters cost 1 less Power to Summon. Ghouls become free (0 Power). This makes Black Goat the most prolific Monster-summoner in the game.

As an Action (1 Power), place a Desecration Marker in an Area where you have the King in Yellow or Hastur, plus enough Undead and Cultists. This also Summons a free Undead. Desecrated Areas generate Power during Gather Power Phase for Yellow Sign.

Pre-Battle ability. A Shoggoth in battle may Absorb (Eliminate) friendly Cultists, Deep Ones, or Starspawn. The Shoggoth adds the Absorbed units' Combat to its own for that battle. Turns a Shoggoth into a devastating combat monster.

Ongoing ability. After any player's Action, replace one of your Cultists with a Deep One from your Pool. This happens reactively, protecting Cultists from Capture and converting them into combat units.

Action. Pay 2 Power to take an enemy Cultist from any Area where they have no Monster or Great Old One protection, and place it in your Pool. The threat of Dreams forces opponents to always guard their Cultists.
Post-Battle ability. Each Starspawn in battle that takes a single Kill result converts it to a Pain instead. Makes Starspawn extremely difficult to eliminate in combat.
Action. Move all your units in an Area to any single ocean Area. Requires Cthulhu to be in play. Enables surprise attacks from the ocean and safe retreats.
Ongoing ability. You may Create Gates in ocean Areas. If an enemy Captures or Takes one of your ocean Gates, you gain 2 Power. Makes ocean Areas a viable base of operations.

Pre-Battle ability. If a Nightgaunt is in the battle, you may Eliminate one enemy Cultist. In the early game, this protects your Gates. In the late game, it is less impactful but still useful for removing Gate controllers.

entries.term: Emissary of the Outer Gods (Crawling Chaos Spellbook)
entries.definition: Post-Battle ability. When Nyarlathotep would be Killed, you may redirect the Kill to a friendly Cultist instead. Protects your expensive Great Old One from death.
Pre-Battle ability. A Flying Polyp in battle can make one unit (on either side) exempt from all battle results. That unit cannot be Killed, Pained, or Eliminated for the duration of the battle.
Post-Battle ability. After any battle on the map (even ones you are not involved in), you choose where all Pained units retreat to. Usable from anywhere. Scatters enemies and disrupts positioning.
Pre-Battle ability. When any battle occurs where you have units, you may add one Hunting Horror from anywhere on the map to that battle for free. Provides instant reinforcement.
Gather Power Phase ability. Roll a die: gain Power equal to the result divided by 2 (rounded up), and each other player loses that much Power. Steals Power from all opponents simultaneously.
Action. Move Shub-Niggurath to any Area on the map, then swap an enemy Cultist from that Area to the Area Shub-Niggurath just left. Enables long-range Gate theft.
Doom Phase ability. Eliminate one of your own Cultists to gain 1 Elder Sign. Provides a steady stream of Elder Signs every Doom Phase at the cost of a Cultist.
Ongoing ability. All of your Cultists have Combat 1 instead of 0. Makes your Cultists legitimate threats in battle and discourages casual attacks.
Action. Roll a die for each Area containing your Fungi from Yuggoth. On certain results, Eliminate enemy Cultists in those Areas. Long-range destruction of enemy infrastructure.

Post-Battle ability. After any battle on the map (even if you are not involved), send Ghouls from your Pool into the battle Area. Each Ghoul adds 1 Pain to both sides. Can swing any battle on the board.
Ongoing ability. Your Dark Young can Control Gates instead of Cultists. Dark Young cannot be Captured, making Red Sign Gates the most secure in the game.

entries.term: He Who is Not to be Named (Yellow Sign Spellbook)
entries.definition: Action. Move Hastur to any Area on the map. Immediately after, you may take a second, different Action. Enables assassination strikes: teleport Hastur, then Battle. Incredibly versatile.
Ongoing ability. Gain 1 Power each time an enemy player Moves or places a unit into an Area where you have units. Generates passive Power from opponent aggression.

Action. After the King in Yellow takes an Action, you may immediately take a second, different Action. Effectively gives you double Actions when the King is involved. One of the most powerful Spellbooks in the game.

entries.term: Shriek of the Byakhee (Yellow Sign Spellbook)
entries.definition: Pre-Battle or Action ability. Add Byakhee from your Pool to an Area containing one of your Great Old Ones. Provides instant combat support for the King in Yellow or Hastur.
Ongoing ability. When you successfully Desecrate an Area while both the King in Yellow and Hastur are in play, gain 1 Elder Sign. Turns Desecration into a steady Elder Sign engine.
Post-Battle ability. When Hastur is in battle, Hastur assigns the enemy's Kill results to enemy units instead of the enemy choosing. Guarantees that the most valuable target is hit.
Action. Eliminate an enemy Acolyte Cultist in any Area where you have an Undead, and place an Undead from your Pool in that Area. Costs 1 Power. Converts enemy Cultists into your Monsters.

A token placed on the map by Yellow Sign's Desecrate ability. Yellow Sign earns Power from Areas with their Desecration Markers during the Gather Power Phase. Also enables Third Eye Elder Sign generation.
Given to the First Player each Determine First Player Phase. Has clockwise and counterclockwise sides to set the direction of play. Great Cthulhu's player always starts with it if in the game.

The track where all players' Doom Markers are placed and advanced. Goes up to 30. When any player reaches 30+, the game end is triggered. Players advance Doom via Controlled Gates and Rituals of Annihilation.
The track showing the current cost of performing a Ritual (5 through 10, then Instant Death). The marker advances each time a Ritual is performed. Different tracks exist for different player counts.
Contains the 36 Elder Sign Trophy Tokens. Players draw from the bag when earning Elder Signs. Tokens are drawn secretly and placed face down near the player's Faction Card.
A quick reference card given to each player during setup. Summarizes key rules and turn structure.

A region on the map. Areas are either ocean or land. Units can freely move between adjacent Areas regardless of type. Each Area can hold at most one Gate. The number of Areas depends on player count (13, 17, or 21).

An ocean region on the map (North Pacific, South Pacific, North Atlantic, South Atlantic, Indian Ocean, Arctic Ocean). Functionally identical to land for most purposes. Cthulhu's Submerge and Y'ha Nthlei interact specifically with ocean Areas.

Two Areas that share a border. Units Move from one Area to an adjacent one. The far right and far left of the map are adjacent because the Earth is round. Retreat from battle must be to an adjacent Area.
Cthulhu's Pre-Battle ability. Eliminate one enemy Cultist or Monster in the battle before dice are rolled. The attacker uses this first, potentially removing a key defender before combat even begins.

Nyarlathotep's Post-Battle ability. When an enemy Great Old One in the same battle is Pained, gain either 2 Power or 2 Elder Signs. Combined with Madness, enables directed Power and Elder Sign farming.
